summary: Matched the ft_0D31 death/rebirth block by splitting the original ft_0D31 object at 0x800D4DD4 into a new matching ft_0D4D.c unit, implementing multiple previously nonmatching common-fighter death and respawn routines, and updating splits, symbols, headers, includes, and build configuration. The decomp.dev report showed +6896 matched code bytes, +10644 linked code bytes, and 34 new matches; its listed broken ft_0D31 matches are mostly functions moved into the new ft_0D4D unit rather than true source loss.

key_files.role: Main decomp target retained for 0x800D3158-0x800D4DD4; added matched death-direction and DeadUpStar/DeadUpFall animation logic and adjusted shared motion-var access.
New split source unit for 0x800D4DD4-0x800D5AEC containing rebirth/death-camera/respawn helper functions.
Removed declarations now owned by ft_0D4D.h and refined ftCo_800D481C prototype.
New header for the split rebirth/death functions.
Split ft_0D31 text/data ranges and inserted ft_0D4D ranges; shifted following ftCo_Attack100 rodata/sdata starts.
Updated local @-style data labels for sdata/sdata2 constants around the new split.
Changed ft_0D31.c from NonMatching to Matching and added ft_0D4D.c as Matching.
Changed ftCommon_MotionVars common fields at fp+2344 from separate x4/x8/xC fields into a Vec3 x4.
Adjusted effect callbacks to use the new Vec3 motion-var layout.
Changed Player_GetUnk45 return type from u8 to u32 for color/index calculations.

what_changed: Split the old ft_0D31 object in config/GALE01/splits.txt from a single 0x800D3158-0x800D5AEC range into ft_0D31.c ending at 0x800D4DD4 and a new ft_0D4D.c covering 0x800D4DD4-0x800D5AEC.

Added src/melee/ft/ft_0D4D.c with implementations for fn_800D4DD4, ftCo_DeadUpFall_Cam, ftCo_800D4E50, ftCo_800D4F24, ftCo_800D4FF4, ftCo_Rebirth_* routines, ftCo_RebirthWait_* routines, fn_800D54A4, fn_800D55B4, fn_800D5A30, and ftCo_Rebirth_Cam.

Implemented or completed several ft_0D31 death animation/death transition routines including ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, ftCo_800D3E40, ftCo_DeadUpStar_Anim, ftCo_800D4580, ftCo_800D481C, and ftCo_DeadUpFall_Anim.
Moved rebirth/death declarations from ft_0D31.h to a new ft_0D4D.h and updated includes in ftCrazyHand, ftMasterHand, ft_0C8C, ftdata, ftlib, ftmaterial, player, and ftmotionstates users.
Changed ftCommon_MotionVars.common at fp+2344 from f32 x4 plus unknown x8/xC members into Vec3 x4, then updated callers to use x4.x, x4.y, and x4.z.
Adjusted eflib effect callbacks: efLib_Cb_SetRotYZ_FromFighter now reads fighter->mv.co.common.x4.x, and Mario/Luigi SpecialLw callbacks test the integer representation at x4.z.
Changed Player_GetUnk45 from returning u8 to u32 and used it in ft_0D31 color lookup logic with an index multiplied by 4.
Updated local data symbols in symbols.txt from ftCo_804D... names to compiler-style @ labels for constants now belonging to the split units.
Marked both melee/ft/ft_0D31.c and melee/ft/ft_0D4D.c as Matching in configure.py.
smart_moves: The object split separated the rebirth block into ft_0D4D.c, allowing the build system and split config to account for distinct text, rodata, sdata, and sdata2 ranges instead of forcing everything through ft_0D31.c.
Moving declarations into ft_0D4D.h kept include dependencies aligned with ownership of the moved functions and avoided leaving rebirth APIs in the earlier ft_0D31 header.
Representing fp+2344/fp+2348/fp+234C as a Vec3 made the rebirth position target and camera-box code much cleaner while still preserving access to individual scalar fields where needed.
The diff used local compiler labels such as @290, @406, and @574 for split-unit constants rather than keeping misleading ftCo_804D... global-looking names.

Several hard-to-match routines used explicit stack padding, scoped temporaries, comma expressions such as (0, temp_r28_2->x4), and #pragma dont_inline to control codegen without changing apparent behavior.

Color packing in death effect spawning was expressed through byte-base pointers and a Player_GetUnk45-derived offset, matching the observed RGB construction rather than using a guessed high-level color struct.
decomp_lessons: When a file boundary cuts through a run of already-known functions, update splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, headers, and includes together; decomp.dev may report moved functions as broken in the old unit and new matches in the new unit.

For common fighter motion vars, grouping adjacent floats/unknowns into Vec3 can unlock cleaner matches across multiple systems, but all existing field users must be audited for x/y/z replacement and type punning.
Camera and respawn logic frequently stores target positions in mv.co.common.x4; after this PR the searchable convention is mv.co.common.x4.x/y/z rather than mv.co.common.x4 plus x8/xC.
Do not assume decomp.dev broken-match tables are semantic regressions when a PR intentionally splits an object; compare unit names and addresses to detect relocated matches.
Small API type changes such as u8 to u32 on Player_GetUnk45 can be matching-relevant because callers may need full-width arithmetic or shifted indexing.
naming_conventions: New split file and header follow address-based names: ft_0D4D.c and ft_0D4D.h for the block starting at 0x800D4DD4.
Function declarations retain address comments such as /* 0D4DD4 */ and unresolved names remain address-style fn_800D4DD4 or ftCo_800D4E50.
Moved rebirth functions use ftCo_Rebirth_* and ftCo_RebirthWait_* names in ft_0D4D.h.
Local data constants in symbols.txt use compiler-generated @NNN labels when they are local sdata/sdata2 literals rather than meaningful exported names.
The shared motion-var field is now named x4 but typed Vec3, with component accesses x4.x, x4.y, and x4.z.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, and ftCo_800D3E40 to control inlining.
Inserted explicit unused stack arrays and PAD_STACK to influence stack frame layout in matched functions.
Used scoped blocks and temporary aliases such as new_var to shape register allocation.
Used type punning (*(s32*) &fighter->mv.co.common.x4.z) for integer tests on a Vec3 component in eflib callbacks.
Used comma expression (0, temp_r28_2->x4) when passing SFX data to ftCo_800D38B8, likely for codegen matching.
Recomputed packed RGB values from Fighter_804D650C/Fighter_804D6508 byte arrays with Player_GetUnk45(player_id) << 2 offsets.
Maintained exact split data ranges for .rodata, .sdata, and .sdata2 to keep later ftCo_Attack100 data addresses correct.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported +0.18% matched code, +6896 matched code bytes, +10644 linked code bytes, and 34 new matches for GALE01.
review_feedback.details: Largest new matches included ftCo_800D4FF4, ftCo_DeadUpFall_Anim, ftCo_DeadUpStar_Anim, ftCo_800D3950, ftCo_800D3BC8, ftCo_800D3E40, ftCo_800D4580, ftCo_800D481C, and multiple ftCo_Rebirth/ftCo_RebirthWait functions.

Evidence indicates these were functions moved into main/melee/ft/ft_0D4D, including ftCo_Rebirth_Phys, fn_800D54A4, ftCo_800D5600, ftCo_800D4E50, ftCo_800D4F24, ftCo_Rebirth_Cam, and related rebirth routines.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments or PR body were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ft_0D31 or ft_0D4D functions from address-style names to semantic names.
Search for remaining uses of mv.co.common.x4.z type punning to see whether ftCommon_MotionVars got a more precise union/struct later.
Check whether Player_GetUnk45 kept u32 return type or was later renamed/documented based on color/team-costume usage.
Review later object-split PRs for the same pattern of decomp.dev broken matches caused by moving functions into a new unit.
Search for subsequent cleanup of #pragma dont_inline, PAD_STACK, comma expressions, or new_var temporaries in ft_0D31/ft_0D4D.
